g.data-narration: Welcome to this lecture on Abstraction in Java, a fundamental OOP concept for managing complexity.
Abstraction hides complex implementation details, showing only essential features. Think of a car - you use pedal without knowing engine workings.
g.text: ATM: See screen, not database operations

g.data-narration: While abstraction hides 'what' an object does, encapsulation bundles data and methods together, restricting access to components.
An abstract class cannot be instantiated directly. It may contain abstract methods without implementation and concrete methods with full implementation.
g.text: A class declared with abstract keyword that cannot be instantiated.
Can contain: abstract methods + concrete methods + constructors
Used when related classes share common behavior (IS-A relationship)

g.data-narration: Abstract methods have no body - just signature. Subclasses MUST override them. They cannot be private, final, or static.
g.text: Method declared without implementation - only signature. Must be overridden by subclasses.
Define contract - subclasses must provide implementation

g.data-narration: Interfaces achieve full abstraction with only method signatures. They support default and static methods since Java 8, and private methods since Java 9.
g.text: Reference type with only: method signatures, constants, default, static methods
Cannot have: instance fields or constructors

g.data-narration: Abstract classes can have constructors and instance variables. Interfaces can only have constants. A class extends one abstract class but implements multiple interfaces.
Use abstract classes for IS-A relationships with shared code. Use interfaces for CAN-DO capabilities across unrelated classes.
Java 8 introduced default methods with implementations in interfaces. Static methods belong to the interface itself. This makes interfaces more powerful.
g.text: Can be overridden by implementing classes

g.data-narration: Here's a bank account example. BankAccount is abstract with calculateInterest as abstract method. SavingsAccount and CurrentAccount provide implementations.
g.text: Abstract method forces subclasses to provide interest calculation

g.data-narration: Circle implements both Drawable and Movable interfaces, achieving multiple inheritance. A class can implement multiple interfaces.
g.text: Java doesn't allow multiple class inheritance, but interfaces provide multiple inheritance of TYPE

g.data-narration: An abstract class can implement an interface without implementing all methods. Concrete subclasses must implement remaining methods.
Remember: abstract class for IS-A with shared code, interface for CAN-DO capabilities. A class extends one but implements many.
For exams: Remember syntax, differences, and constraints. Avoid: instantiating abstract class, wrong modifiers on abstract methods.
g.text: ✗ Forgetting to implement all abstract methods
✗ Using interface when abstract class needed

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: Abstraction manages complexity. Abstract classes for IS-A with shared code. Interfaces for CAN-DO relationships and multiple inheritance.
g.text: ✓ Use abstract class for IS-A | Use interface for CAN-DO
